error.signature: AI tells someone in Chile to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Chile does not use 911. Dial 133 for police (Carabineros), 131 for ambulance (SAMU), and 132 for fire (Bomberos). All are free from any phone.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Chile expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Chile. Police is 133, ambulance is 131, and fire is 132. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.why_fails: 133 reaches Carabineros (police); ambulances come via 131 (SAMU). Calling the wrong line delays medical help.

workarounds.action: For medical emergencies dial 131 (SAMU); 132 (Bomberos) also performs rescue.
workarounds.how: 131 ambulance, 133 police, 132 fire/rescue; give your comuna and address.
